name: SpecKit
description: GitHub's spec-driven development toolkit for AI-assisted development workflows

description.value: GitHub's open-source toolkit for spec-driven development that drives AI coding agents through a structured Constitution → Specify → Plan → Tasks → Implement workflow.

first-release.comment: Publicly announced 2025-09-02 on the GitHub blog; repo created 2025-08-21.

latest-release.comment: v0.7.3 published 2026-04-17 (verified via gh API on 2026-04-20).

covered-phases.comment: Maps to slash commands: /speckit.constitution (architecture), /speckit.specify (spec), /speckit.clarify & /speckit.plan (planning), /speckit.tasks (tasks), /speckit.implement (implementation), /speckit.analyze (review). No distinct testing phase — TDD is prescribed via the constitution but not a separate step.

methodology-style.comment: The spec is the central, executable artifact that generates code; plan-and-execute fits the phase progression.

opinionated.comment: Phases are ordered; /speckit.tasks requires plan.md; constitution enforces architectural principles across generation.

ceremony-level.comment: Multiple mandated artifacts per feature (constitution, spec, plan, tasks, contracts, data-model, research) plus review gates push beyond 'medium'.

mechanization-level.comment: Specify CLI plus slash commands wrap each phase, but the user drives progression manually command-by-command rather than a single end-to-end run.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: SDD doc mandates team review of specs and explicit test approval before implementation.

approval-gates.comment: Default workflow surfaces each artifact for human review before advancing; implementation runs after tasks are approved.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Default configuration mandates human review at multiple phases; not pitched as a hands-off pipeline.

autonomy-level.comment: Phase-level approvals on spec / plan / tasks; each slash command runs end-to-end within its phase.

rollback-support.comment: No built-in revert mechanism documented; users rely on git branches.

supported-agents.comment: README advertises 30+ agents including GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor CLI, Codex CLI, Qwen, Mistral Vibe, Tabnine, Kiro; 'other' covers the rest that don't have a dedicated tag.

editor-integrations.comment: SpecKit itself is CLI/slash-command based; editor integrations come from the underlying agent, not from SpecKit.

model-agnostic.comment: Works with any supported agent, which in turn runs against any backing model.

mcp-support.comment: MCP server proposed in issue #99 but marked stale and unimplemented as of 2026-04-20.

artifact-formats.comment: spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, constitution.md, research.md, data-model.md are all markdown.

artifacts-covered.comment: Constitution covers architecture/principles; data-model and contracts are architecture-adjacent. No built-in release-notes, changelog, or roadmap artifacts.

project-layout.value: .specify/ with memory/constitution.md, specs/<FEATURE-ID>/{spec,plan,tasks,data-model,research}.md and contracts/, plus templates/, extensions/, presets/, scripts/

artifacts-in-git.comment: Everything lives under .specify/ in the repo.

language-agnostic.comment: No language-specific assumptions; tech stack is chosen in the plan phase.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Core enforcement is prompt/constitution text instructing the agent. CI gates (e.g. 'CI Guard') exist as community extensions but are not part of the default workflow.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: /speckit.tasks requires plan.md (required); phases are sequentially dependent.

artifact-validation.comment: Artifacts are markdown templates; no schema validation on structure or content.

memory-implementation.comment: Constitution and spec artifacts are kept under .specify/memory/ and .specify/specs/ as markdown.

persistent-memory.comment: Artifacts are committed to the repo and consulted across sessions.

project-context-files.comment: constitution.md plus per-agent context files (e.g. AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) are produced during init.

cost-tracking.comment: No built-in token or cost telemetry; the host agent handles any such reporting.

run-history.comment: No framework-level run history beyond git history of the generated artifacts.

telemetry-opt.comment: No telemetry collection disclosed or referenced; attribute is not meaningfully applicable to a set of slash commands and CLI scaffolder.

custom-templates.comment: Three-tier template resolution: project overrides, presets, extensions, plus defaults.

hooks.comment: No first-class pre/post-phase hook system in core. Some community extensions (e.g. CI Guard) add gating behavior, but that is not a general lifecycle-hook mechanism.

sub-agents.comment: Extensions and presets function as reusable personas/workflows; --ai-skills flag registers SpecKit commands as agent skills.

marketplace.comment: Community extensions and presets catalog at speckit-community.github.io/extensions/.

install-mechanism.comment: Primary install is `uv tool install specify-cli` / `uvx` from the git repo (Python-packaged CLI). Air-gapped flows use `pip download`.

setup-time.comment: One `uvx` command scaffolds a project via `specify init`.

requires-account.comment: SpecKit itself requires no login; the backing AI agent may have its own auth.

git-worktree-support.comment: Community extensions 'Worktrees' and 'Worktree Isolation' are advertised for parallel feature work; not mandatory.

documentation-quality.comment: Dedicated doc site, long-form spec-driven.md manifesto, blog launch post, extensive CLI help, extension catalog; gaps around telemetry/rollback.

active-maintenance.comment: Latest push 2026-04-20 and v0.7.3 released 2026-04-17 — well within 90 days.

example-projects.comment: LinkedIn Learning course repo and blog walkthroughs; community extensions directory also serves as usage examples.

community-channel.comment: Routes to GitHub Community Discussions; no dedicated Discord/Slack mentioned.
